No Poverty.reason: The course discusses economic impacts and distribution of benefits, which are better captured by SDGs 8 and 10, rather than direct poverty eradication measures of SDG 1.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: The course explicitly states AI will manage 'healthcare' and '健康照護與保險,' directly linking to the provision and management of health services (Target 3.d).

Quality Education.reason: The course itself is tertiary education (Target 4.3), aims to equip students with future skills (Target 4.4), and fosters an international learning environment for understanding global impacts (Target 4.7).

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course is fundamentally about AI as a 'technological revolution' and its impact on 'infrastructure' (基礎設備), directly addressing innovation (Target 9.5) and resilient infrastructure (Target 9.1).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course explicitly questions whether the 'benefits of this technological revolution be broadly distributed or accrue to a lucky few,' directly addressing the core issue of reducing inequalities and ensuring fair distribution (Target 10.2, 10.3).

Climate Action.reason: The general mention of AI managing 'our environment' is insufficient to establish a direct or strong inferred link to specific climate action targets like resilience, policy integration, or awareness.

Life Below Water.reason: No explicit evidence or strong inferred links regarding oceans, marine life, pollution, or fisheries were found in the course description.

Life on Land.reason: No explicit evidence or strong inferred links regarding terrestrial ecosystems, forests, biodiversity, or land degradation were found in the course description.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course title 'AI & Governance' and its explicit focus on 'legal rights and responsibilities,' 'ethical principles,' '法律制度,' and '公、私部門治理' make it a direct and strong fit for SDG 16 (Targets 16.3, 16.6).
